name: Event 201
description: Event 201 was a high-level simulation of a fictional coronavirus pandemic held on October 18, 2019, in New York City. It was a collaboration between the Johns Hopkins Center for Health Security, the World Economic Forum, and the Bill & Melinda Gates Foundation. The exercise simulated the global response to a novel zoonotic coronavirus transmitted from bats to pigs to humans that eventually becomes efficiently transmissible from person to person, leading to a severe pandemic. Proponents of conspiracy theories often cite Event 201 as evidence that the COVID-19 pandemic was pre-planned or that these organizations had foreknowledge of the outbreak. They point to the simulation's accurate predictions of societal disruptions, economic impacts, and the need for coordinated global responses as proof of a sinister agenda. Mainstream perspectives, however, emphasize that Event 201 was a public health preparedness exercise designed to identify weaknesses in pandemic response strategies. Organizers have stated that the simulation was intended to raise awareness and improve preparedness for potential pandemics, and that its timing was coincidental to the actual COVID-19 outbreak. Critics of the conspiracy theories highlight that pandemic simulations are common practice in public health and that the exercise's outcomes were based on established epidemiological models and historical pandemic data. The accuracy of some predictions is attributed to the inherent nature of pandemics and the likelihood of certain consequences regardless of specific pathogen.
subtitle: Pandemic simulation and conspiracy narratives

claims.claim: Event 201 proves the COVID-19 pandemic was pre-planned.
claims.evidence: The simulation accurately predicted many aspects of the COVID-19 pandemic, including societal disruptions and economic impacts.
claims.criticism: The simulation was a preparedness exercise based on established epidemiological models and historical data. The accuracy of predictions is due to the inherent nature of pandemics.

claims.claim: The organizations involved in Event 201 (Johns Hopkins, WEF, Gates Foundation) orchestrated the pandemic for their own gain.
claims.evidence: These organizations have vested interests in pharmaceutical companies and global health initiatives.
claims.criticism: These organizations are involved in public health research and initiatives, and their participation in the simulation does not imply involvement in the pandemic's origin or spread.

claims.claim: Event 201 was a 'script' for the COVID-19 pandemic, dictating the responses of governments and institutions.
claims.evidence: The simulation outlined potential responses such as lockdowns, mask mandates, and vaccine development, which were later implemented during the pandemic.
claims.criticism: These responses are standard public health measures recommended during pandemics and were not unique to the simulation. The simulation served as a basis for discussion and not a set of instructions.

keyFigures.name: Bill Gates
keyFigures.role: Co-chair of the Bill & Melinda Gates Foundation, a participant in Event 201

keyFigures.name: Eric Toner
keyFigures.role: Senior Scientist at the Johns Hopkins Center for Health Security, lead organizer of Event 201

keyFigures.name: David Icke
keyFigures.role: Conspiracy theorist who promoted the idea that Event 201 was a pre-planned pandemic

mythologyConnections: Prophecies of doom and societal collapse
